music: cinematic documentary score evolving from ancient organic hand percussion to a modern electronic pulse, driving yet reflective, instrumental, no vocals

beats.narration: For most of history, there was no money. You traded a goat for grain, and hoped the other person actually wanted a goat.

shots.scene: two ancient traders facing off in a barter, a cut-out goat, sacks of grain, a scatter of cowrie shells, a scratched clay tablet, a torn arrow scrap between them
shots.motion: one slow gentle push-in on the whole collage; layered paper elements drift with parallax; halftone pulses softly
shots.keyframe_prompt: Mixed-media hand-cut PAPER COLLAGE, editorial motion-graphic zine style. Clearly layered paper cut-outs with visible torn and scissor-cut edges, tape corners and soft real paper drop shadows, on a bold flat earthy clay tan paper background. Halftone print dots, newspaper-clipping scraps, paper-stencil shapes, aged paper texture, slight print misregistration. Scattered geometric collage accents (a small blue paper triangle, a red circle, a zigzag, halftone strips, bits of washi tape). Figures are PRINTED-texture cut-outs of real imagery (sepia old photographs / Chinese woodblock-print / mural), NOT CGI, NOT a 3D render — keep print grain and paper imperfections. High-contrast, punchy, tactile, hand-assembled. SCENE (as layered paper cut-outs): two ancient traders facing off in a barter, a cut-out goat, sacks of grain, a scatter of cowrie shells, a scratched clay tablet, a torn arrow scrap between them. Include a torn-paper banner with a big bold cut-out English headline 'BEFORE MONEY', plus a single red seal stamp. Keep the headline crisp and legible. Aspect ratio 9:16.

beats.narration: So people found a shortcut. Shells, then metal. Around 600 BC, the kingdom of Lydia struck the very first coins.

shots.scene: a string of cowrie shells, a heap of gold, a big stamped ancient Lydian coin as the focal cut-out, an ancient hand reaching in, halftone sunburst rays
shots.motion: one slow gentle push-in on the whole collage; layered paper elements drift with parallax; halftone pulses softly

beats.narration: A thousand years ago, Song-dynasty China did something radical: money made of paper. A promise you could fold into your pocket.

shots.scene: a Song-dynasty jiaozi paper banknote as the hero cut-out, a Chinese scholar-official, a bright red ink seal stamp, a stack of round coins turning into a folded paper note
shots.motion: one slow gentle push-in on the whole collage; layered paper elements drift with parallax; halftone pulses softly

beats.narration: Paper needed guardians. Banks rose. Then plastic, and suddenly your money was just a number inside a machine.

shots.motion: one slow gentle push-in on the whole collage; layered paper elements drift with parallax; halftone pulses softly

beats.narration: Then money left the physical world entirely. Screens. Apps. And in 2009, Bitcoin. Cash with no bank, no border, no paper.

shots.scene: a glowing smartphone showing a payment, a bright bitcoin coin as the focal cut-out, a web of glowing network nodes, streams of ones and zeros, neon cyan and orange accents on a dark ground
shots.motion: one slow gentle push-in on the whole collage; layered paper elements drift with parallax; halftone pulses softly

beats.narration: From shells to code, money never truly changed. It was always the same thing: a story we all agree to believe.
